w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Brand Custom Request Use Case Document
Use Case 15: Brand Custom Nail Request Scenarios

This document provides a comprehensive overview of Use Case 15: Brand Custom Request for nail services. It details thirteen distinct scenarios encompassing how a brand company submits custom nail requests, the flows for client and artist selection, request status changes, and interface elements supporting these workflows. The intended audience includes product managers, developers, and QA teams, ensuring clarity and actionable guidance for implementation.

The purpose of this document is to define the operational and technical requirements for Brand Custom Request scenarios, focusing on the interactions between brand companies, clients, and artists. The scope includes request submission, selection logic, workflow states, user actions (Accept/Cancel), and order status updates.
Submits custom nail requests and selects client and artist pools or specific entities.
Receives requests, reviews details, accepts or declines, and interacts via provided interface elements.
Reviews accepted requests from clients or brand companies, accepts or declines assignments, and updates order status.
Use Case 1- Open to Client and Open to Artist
Scenario 1.1 - Open to Client and Artist Pool, Client accepts the request and artist accepts. (Single order)
Scenario 1.2 - Open to Client and Artist Pool, Client declines the request and request expires. (Single order)
Scenario 1.3 - Open to Client and Artist Pool, a client declines but another client accepts, no artist accepts and
Scenario 1.4 - Open to Client and Artist Pool, Client accepts the request and before artist accepts, client
Scenario 1.5 - Open to Client and Artist Pool, Client accepts the request and before artist accepts, Brand cancels the request. (Single order)
Scenario 1.6 - Open to Client and Artist Pool, request expires. (Single order)
Use Case 2- Open to Client and Specific Artist
Scenario 2.1 - Open to Client and Specific Artist (Direct to Artist), Client accepts the request and artist accepts. (Single order)
Scenario 2.2 - Open to Client and Specific Artist (Direct to Artist), Client accepts the request and artist declines, goes to artist pool. (Single order)
Scenario 2.3 - Open to Client and Specific Artist (Direct to Artist), Client accepts the request and before artist accepts, Brand cancels the request. (Single order)
Scenario 2.4 - Open to Client and Specific Artist (Direct to Artist), Client accepts the request and request expires. (Single order)
Use Case 3- Specific Client and Open to Artist Pool
Scenario 3.1 - Specific Client and Artist Pool, Direct client accepts the request and artist accepts (Single order)
Scenario 3.2 - Specific Client and Artist Pool, Direct client declines the request, goes to client pool, client from pool accepts the request and artist accepts (Single order)
Scenario 3.3 - Specific Client and Artist Pool, Direct client declines the request, goes to client pool and no client accepts and
Scenario 3.4 - Specific Client and Artist Pool, Direct client accepts the request and brand cancel before artist accepts (Single order)
Scenario 3.5 - Specific Client and Artist Pool, and request expires (Single order)
Use Case 4- Designate Specific Client, Request Specific Artist
Scenario 4.1 - Specific Client and specific Artist, Direct client accepts the request and artist accepts (Single order)
Scenario 4.2 - Specific Client and specific Artist, Direct client declines the request, goes to client pool, client from pool accepts and direct artist accepts (Single order)
Scenario 4.3 - Specific Client and specific Artist, Direct client declines the request, goes to client pool, client from pool accepts and direct artist declines, goes to artist pool (Single order)
Scenario 4.4 - Specific Client and specific Artist, Direct client accepts the
Scenario 4.5 - Specific Client and specific Artist, Direct client accepts the request and brand cancel before artist accepts (Single order)
Scenario 4.6 - Specific Client and specific Artist, Direct client accepts the request and expires before artist accepts (Single order)
Use Case 5- Group Client, Open to Artist pool
Scenario 5.1 - Group Client (2+ client) and open to Artist pool,
client accepts the request, goes to artist pool and artist accepts (Group order)
Scenario 5.2 - Group Client (2+ client) and open to Artist pool, few clients decline and few clients accepts the request, goes to artist pool and artist accepts (Group order) – 10 clients
Scenario 5.3 - Group Client (2+ client) and open to Artist pool, clients accept the request,
to artist pool and request is in pool even when an artist declines and expires (Group order)

Scenario 5.4 - Group Client (2+ client) and open to Artist pool, few clients decline and few clients accept the request and other gets expired, accepted clients go to artist pool and artist accepts (Group order) – 15 clients
Scenario 5.5 - Group Client (2+ client) and open to Artist pool, few
Brand cancel the request (Group order) – 5 clients
Scenario 5.6 - Group Client (2+ client) and open to Artist pool, few clients decline and few clients accept the request, goes to artists pool, Brand cancel the request (Group order) – 5 clients
Use Case 6- Group Client, Specific Artist
Scenario 6.1 - Group Client (2+ client) and Specific Artist pool,
client accepts the request, goes to direct artist and artist accepts (Group order)
Scenario 6.2 - Group Client (2+ client) and Specific Artist, few clients decline and few clients accepts the request, goes to direct artist and artist accepts (Group order) – 10 clients
Scenario 6.3 - Group Client (2+ client) and Specific Artist, clients accept the request, goes to direct artist and expires (Group order)

Scenario 6.4 - Group Client (2+ client) and Specific artist, few clients decline and few clients accept the request and other gets expired, accepted clients go to direct artist and artist accepts (Group order) – 15 clients

Scenario 6.5 - Group Client (2+ client) and Specific artist, few clients decline and few clients accept the request and other gets expired, accepted clients go to direct artist, artist declines and goes to pool (Group order) – 15 clients
Scenario 6.6 - Group Client (2+ client) and Specific
accept the request, Brand cancel the request (Group order) – 5 clients
Scenario 6.7 - Group Client (2+ client) and specific artist, few clients decline and few clients accept the request, goes to direct artist, Brand cancel the request (Group order) – 5 clients
- Open to Client and Open to Artist
Open to Client and Artist Pool

Client (All client select) accepts the brand
Brand company gets the notification ‘{Client name} has accepted your {Campaign name} brand request {order #}’
Admin get the notification ‘{Client name} has accepted the {Brand Company} {Campaign name} brand request {order #}’
Artist (Pool) gets the notification ‘You have received a Brand request {order #} for {client name} from {Brand name} {Campaign Name}’

Artist (Direct only) gets the notification ‘{Brand Company} cancelled {Campaign name} brand request
Client accepted, artist review in progress.
Client or artist declined the request.
Request canceled by user or brand company.
by user actions (Accept, Decline, Cancel), system expiry, or cancellation events.
Allows client or artist to accept the request.
Enables user to cancel the request.
For selecting clients, artists, or groups during request submission.
Displays request information, status, and action buttons for all parties.
This document defines the end-to-end workflow logic for Brand Custom Request scenarios, covering

distinct flows for client and artist selection, status updates, and user actions. The next steps include validating these scenarios with stakeholders, refining interface designs, and preparing for development and QA implementation.
